docstring:
/**
 * @brief Module destructor helper that runs global C++/C destructors exactly once.
 *
 * Invoked by the runtime (registered via `.fini_array` / `__do_global_dtors_aux_fini_array_entry`)
 * during shared-object unload or program termination. The function is idempotent: it
 * uses the file-scope guard variable `completed.0` to ensure the cleanup body executes
 * at most once, regardless of how many times it is called.
 *
 * On the first invocation it:
 *  - Calls `__cxa_finalize(__dso_handle)` (when the symbol is resolved, i.e. non-NULL)
 *    so that any destructors registered via `__cxa_atexit` for this DSO are run.
 *  - Calls `deregister_tm_clones()` to tear down transactional-memory clone tables
 *    that were set up by the matching constructor aux routine.
 *  - Sets `completed.0` to 1 to suppress subsequent executions.
 *
 * @note This function is generated automatically by the toolchain (crtbegin/crtend
 *       glue). It takes no parameters and returns no value. It must not be called
 *       directly by user code.
 */
assumptions: Standard GCC-emitted __do_global_dtors_aux boilerplate; recognized flag at [var0+0x4150] as the `completed.0` guard.
Recognized call to 0x11a0 as PLT stub for __cxa_finalize, passed __dso_handle (loaded from [var0+0x4008]).

The check against the constant 0x3fd000000000 is the decompiler's misrendering of a NULL test on __cxa_finalize (weak symbol); collapsed both branches which both call deregister_tm_clones and set the flag.
Dropped push(rbp)/epilogue as pure calling-convention artifacts.
divergences: [low] other: Original compares loaded qword at [var0+0x3fd0] against literal 0x3fd000000000, which is a decompiler rendering artifact of a RIP-relative NULL test on the weak __cxa_finalize symbol. Rewrite treats this as a plain NULL check, which is the semantically correct interpretation.
[low] dropped_side_effect: push(rbp) and rbp restore dropped — pure ABI artifacts with no observable effect.
